raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed a {$390.00} charge from XXXX XXXX XXXX which posted to my CitiBank credit card in XX/XX/XXXX. This card is white-label branded as BestBuy Visa . 
- the {$390.00} was for a financial plan that XXXX Advisors were to provide - In late XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted the XXXX  rep to ask if the plan is near completion - The XXXX  rep advised should be complete by mid XX/XX/XXXX - In late XX/XX/XXXX, after not receiving it, I advised the rep to cancel the order - The rep responded to confirm he would not deliver and should cancel - The rep then advised he would issue a refund - The refund did not occur so in XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed the original charge ( attached letter ) - Through the Best Buy Visa website, Citibank proceeded to request information from me beginning on XX/XX/XXXX ( attached letter ) - Through the Best Buy Visa website, I twice submitted a statement on the issue ( attached ) - Through the Best Buy Visa website, I uploaded email proof to my claim ( attached ) - On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Citibank/BestBuyVisa stating they could not verify that I had a billing error. ( attached ) - On XX/XX/XXXX, I called customer service to advise that the dispute was not for a billing error, but instead a service that was not delivered - The customer service rep advised that he is not able to see all the documents pertaining to my claim, so I advised that I would not waste either of our time... as I have uploaded information twice, but evidently, it's either not retrievable, not reviewed, or the team is too overwhelmed to handle even the most clear-cut case of a dispute. 
- I advised the customer service rep that I would refer the issue to a more capable organization, the CFPB - Attached are the letters from CitiBank/BestBuyVisa and my responses to those letters outlining the issue and providing email proof/confirmation of undelivered service
